latest.title: Form 8.5 (EPT/RI) IQE PLC
latest.content:
16 Mar 2026 12:00
RNS Number : 7965W
Peel Hunt LLP
16 March 2026
FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)
PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITH RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY STATUS DEALING IN A CLIENT-SERVING CAPACITY
Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Name of exempt principal trader:
Peel Hunt LLP
(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
IQE PLC
(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:
IQE PLC
(d) Date dealing undertaken:
13 March 2026
(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to this offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"
N/A
2. DEALINGS BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchases/ sales
Total number of securities
Highest price per unit paid/received
Lowest price per unit paid/received
Ordinary Shares
Purchases
2,255,919
24.55 GBx
21.83 GBx
Ordinary Shares
Sales
2,229,211
24.54 GBx
22.24 GBx
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit
Type
e.g. American, European etc.
Expiry date
Option money paid/ received per unit
(ii) Exercise
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Exercising/ exercised against
Number of securities
Exercise price per unit
(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security
Nature of dealing
e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
3. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
Date of disclosure:
16 March 2026
Contact name:
Lesah Munyakazi
Telephone number:
+44 (0) 20 7418 8900
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service. The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's dealing disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.

.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. No

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

company_30s.what_they_do: IQE plc makes and sells photonic devices that help make high‑speed computer chips. Their core products are photonic integrated circuits (PICs) and related components that allow data to move faster and more efficiently inside chips.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The semiconductor industry is moving toward ever faster and smaller chips, and photonics is the key to keeping data traffic from bottlenecking. IQE’s technology can plug into the supply chain of major chipmakers, giving it a strategic niche in a market that is expanding as 5G, AI and cloud computing grow.
company_30s.current_state: IQE is still struggling to turn a profit and is running on limited cash. The business has seen a sharp slide in share price, and it is trading near the lower end of its valuation range. The company is focused on keeping its operations afloat while it seeks new contracts or a potential buyer.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy filing quarterly reports and regulatory updates. On 10 March the board released a financial summary (Form 8.5) and a Rule 2.9 notice, both of which simply confirm the status quo rather than announcing any breakthrough.

Earlier in March the company disclosed changes in director shareholdings and voting rights, signalling that the ownership structure is being tightened, possibly in preparation for a sale or a new round of capital.
The February 27 filing (Form 8.3) was another routine earnings report, again with no headline‑making news.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silently hopeful – a handful of traders whisper about a rebound, but most voices are absent.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of traders are betting on a 50p target, citing technical support levels and potential future contracts.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others fear the company’s cash burn and management issues. They see a takeover as the only exit, and worry that the business cannot recover without external funding.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is largely static – a few bullish posts appear after earnings, but they are quickly drowned out by bearish concerns. The mood is cautiously skeptical.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for IQE is flat, indicating that the wider market is not tracking the company. A sudden news event could therefore generate a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The next earnings release could reveal a new contract or an improvement in cash flow. If the company reports a positive operating margin or a sizeable order book, that would be a trigger.
Any announcement of a partnership with a major chipmaker, or a new EUV equipment deal, would signal that demand for IQE’s photonics is picking up.
A takeover bid or a fresh funding round would immediately lift the share price, as investors would see the company’s survival prospects improving.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume spike that exceeds the 20‑day average, a breakout above the 50‑day moving average, or a clear change in the earnings narrative. A sudden change in the director shareholding pattern or a new regulatory approval would also be key.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the late stages of a capitulation cycle – the price is near its lowest point, and the market is largely silent. A mean‑reversion play would require a clear catalyst to move the price out of the current support zone.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the deep decline is still present, but without a concrete catalyst the upside is uncertain.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If IQE secures a significant contract or a takeover offer, the stock could climb to the mid‑range of its 52‑week range, delivering a 30–50% upside from the current price. A deep crash bottom historically has produced large rebounds when smart money starts accumulating, so a single catalyst could unlock value.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s thin cash position and ongoing losses mean that a failure to secure new funding or contracts could push the price lower. A realistic downside could be a further 20–30% drop if the market decides the business is unsalvageable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 30–50% against a downside risk of 20–30%. The asymmetry is modest – the trade is not a guaranteed big win, but it is not a guaranteed crash either.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after a clear breakout above the 50‑day moving average with volume at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Position size should be 10–15% of your discretionary account, with a stop just below the recent low to limit loss to ~25% of the trade.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the next earnings release. If the company reports a positive earnings surprise or a new contract, consider a small position with a tighter stop. If a takeover bid is announced, move to a larger position immediately.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent terminal risks such as bankruptcy filings or delisting notices. Therefore, the default stance remains neutral until a catalyst emerges.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IQE’s share price sits at a deep trough with no clear catalyst; a single positive development could trigger a modest rebound, but the risk of further decline remains.

Historical data shows 2 rallies averaging 65% upside. Current position: -22.3%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.

bear_case: TOO_EARLY means you can get chopped before real move
